todaysEvents.summary: Iran successor government makes back-channel contact via Oman; US rejects direct talks pending verifiable Hormuz mine-clearance. Crude tanker operators confirm transit halt — est. 17Mbpd offline. VLCC war risk insurance premiums at 3.5% of hull value/voyage (Mar 3)

Singapore MAS issues emergency circular to all financial institutions on expanded Iran sanctions compliance; SGX suspends trading in 2 ASEAN energy-sector names pending regulatory review of Iran-linked counterparty exposure (Mar 3)
Saudi Arabia and UAE announce maximum spare capacity activation — additional 1.5Mbpd pledged to offset Hormuz disruption; 3-week ramp timeline expected before volumes reach market (Mar 3)

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Hormuz disruption lasts 2–4 weeks; Iran successor government enters ceasefire via Oman back-channel. Brent stabilises at $78–85/bbl with sustained risk premium. Saudi/UAE spare capacity partially offsets supply deficit within 3 weeks. ASEAN LNG buyers scramble for spot cargoes; JKM peaks near $18/MMBtu. SCS tensions remain elevated but secondary to Iran shock. Goldman Q4 2026 normalisation forecast at $60/bbl contingent on swift resolution.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Hormuz closure extends 1–3 months; Iran proxy forces activate across Gulf. Brent surges toward $100/bbl (BloombergNEF $91 target). Saudi/UAE ramp delayed by infrastructure constraints. JKM LNG spikes to ~$25/MMBtu — worst gas market disruption since Russia-Ukraine 2022. ASEAN current account deficits widen sharply; PHP and THB most vulnerable. Bank Indonesia forced into emergency intervention as IDR breaches historic lows.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Full Hormuz closure sustained beyond 3 months simultaneously with SCS kinetic escalation. Oil at $150–200/bbl (J.P. Morgan upper bound). LNG effectively unavailable for spot buyers; Qatar's 77 mtpa offline. Saudi/UAE spare capacity absorbed and unable to compensate. Recession probability >70% for Thailand and Philippines. ASEAN EM bonds face mass outflows; 1970s-style energy shock across the region.
